Vulnerabilities.Title: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack (CONTINUATION Flood)
Vulnerabilities.Description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly.

Vulnerabilities.Title: zlib: heap-based buffer over-read and overflow via large gzip header extra field
Vulnerabilities.Description: zlib through 1.2.12 has a heap-based buffer over-read or buffer overflow in inflate in inflate.c via a large gzip header extra field. NOTE: only applications that call inflateGetHeader are affected.

Vulnerabilities.Title: openssl: Possible DoS translating ASN.1 object identifiers
Vulnerabilities.Description: Issue summary: Processing some specially crafted ASN.1 object identifiers or data containing them may be very slow. Impact summary: Applications that use OBJ_obj2txt() directly, or use any of the OpenSSL subsystems including X.509, PKCS#12, CMS, OCSP, PKIX certificates, certificate revocation lists (CRLs), private or public key encoding may be affected.

Vulnerabilities.Title: werkzeug: high resource usage when parsing multipart form data with many fields
Vulnerabilities.Description: Werkzeug prior to 2.2.3 and 2.3.3 handles resource consumption for multipart/form-data parsing. Applications using Werkzeug are susceptible to denial of service via crafted requests.

Vulnerabilities.Title: flask: possible disclosure of permanent session cookie due to missing Vary: Cookie header
Vulnerabilities.Description: Flask before 2.3.2 and 2.2.x before 2.2.5 may disclose a session cookie to users of a shared cache. A response containing data intended for one client may be cached and subsequently sent by the cache to other users.

Vulnerabilities.Title: requests: unintended leak of Proxy-Authorization header
Vulnerabilities.Description: Requests forwards proxy-authorization headers to destination servers when following redirects, potentially leaking credentials.
